business.headline: Gold is rising slowly: Should you expect a big move soon?Gold has been moving up, but quietly. There's no sudden surge, just a gradual rise. So the big question is—does this signal a strong rally ahead or more sideways movement?

IMF warns Asia may face sharper hit from Middle East energy shockAsia relies heavily on imported oil and gas, particularly from the Middle East. This makes it more vulnerable when supply disruptions or price spikes occur.

Gold, equities or debt? Expert says no asset stays on top for longIf you're waiting to invest in the "best" asset, you may be chasing a moving target. An expert explains why gold, equities and debt keep rotating as top performers over time.

NPS, EPF or mutual funds: Where should you invest Rs 5,000 for 10 yearsYou set aside Rs 5,000 every month with one simple goal, i.e., to build something meaningful over 10 years. But the real question is where to invest it. NPS, EPF or mutual funds? Let's have a closer look.

Sensex slips 123 points, Nifty ends below 24,200 as markets erase early gainsThe BSE Sensex closed at 77,988.68, down 122.56 points or 0.16%. The NSE Nifty 50 settled at 24,196.75, lower by 34.55 points or 0.14%.

Sensex falls 1,000 points after early gains: What should investors do?The BSE Sensex moved within a wide range of 1,055.39 points, touching a high of 78,730.32 and a low of 77,674.93. The NSE Nifty 50 also saw similar movement, trading in a range of 298.15 points between a high of 24,400.95 and a low of 24,102.80.

Income below exemption limit? These 3 transactions make ITR filing compulsoryNo taxable income doesn't always mean no ITR. Certain transactions can bring you under the tax net, even if your earnings are below the basic limit.

From Rs 50 to Rs 200: How you can save up to Rs 1.05 lakh with meal vouchersWhat if your daily lunch could help you save tax? With the limit rising from Rs 50 to Rs 200 per meal, meal vouchers are suddenly becoming a powerful tax-saving tool for salaried employees.

DA hike has been delayed, but will you get it this year?If you've been waiting for the DA hike announcement, you might have noticed the silence this time. It hasn't come when expected, but does that mean trouble, or just a delay?

Workforce shrinks, pay hikes normalise: How TCS is reshaping hiring amid AI boomTCS has restored its salary cycle, but with more selective hiring and differentiated pay, signalling a shift towards skills and performance as the key drivers of workforce growth.

Mark Mobius dies at 89: The investor who took global money to emerging marketsMark Mobius built his reputation by investing in emerging markets that many investors once avoided due to political risk, weak governance and currency volatility. His approach helped draw billions of dollars into these regions.

8th Pay Commission: Will Rs 69,000 minimum pay actually happen?The proposal, submitted by the staff side of the National Council–Joint Consultative Machinery, has quickly grabbed attention for the scale of the increase it seeks.
Sensex opens 400 points higher, Nifty above 24,300; IT stocks upThe S&P BSE Sensex added 550.07 points to 78,661.31, while the NSE Nifty50 was up by 158.10 points to 24,389.40 as of 9:27 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market continue its rally or fall today?Early indicators suggest a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,317.5 at 7:59 am, pointing to an opening above Wednesday's close of 24,231.3 for the Nifty 50.

TCS filing made easier: Income Tax Dept brings in new Form 143, replacing 27EQThe move is part of a wider effort to simplify tax processes under the updated income-tax framework and reduce errors in filing.

Indian delegation to visit US from April 20 as trade talks resumeAn Indian delegation led by the chief negotiator will visit the US from April 20. This will be the first in-person meeting between the two sides in about three to four months.
Sensex ends 1,264 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; IT stocks jumpThe S&P BSE Sensex jumped 1,263.67 points to close at 78,111.24, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 38.65 points to end at 24,231.30.

Is India's gold loan boom quietly building a default risk?Gold loans have grown nearly 3.8 times since March 2022. Their share in the retail credit market has also increased sharply, rising from 5.9% to 11.1% by December 2025. This makes gold loans the second-largest retail loan category after home loans.

DA hike update: Central government staff to protest on April 16Weeks after it was expected, the DA hike for January 2026 is still pending. With no update from the government, central government employee body is preparing to protest on April 16.

Zero tax to pay? 3 big reasons why you should still file your ITRIf your income is low and your taxes come to zero, you might think filing an ITR is not needed. But skipping it could mean missing out on some useful benefits that go beyond just paying tax.

entertainment.headline: Gaurav Gera: Aditya Dhar handles pressure with ease, has a no-chaos work style Gaurav Gera praised Dhurandhar director Aditya Dhar's calm and composed nature while working on the blockbuster film. He shared how Dhar's evolved mindset and easygoing approach on set left a lasting impression on him.

Victoria Beckham breaks silence, avoids Brooklyn's mention in cryptic comment The Beckham family drama just won't die down. Victoria Beckham has now subtly weighed in on her fallout with son Brooklyn in a new interview without naming him.
Meghan Markle joins MasterChef Australia as guest judge. Watch promo Meghan Markle will appear as a guest judge on MasterChef Australia Season 18, marking her return to Australia after 2018.

Kangana Ranaut recalls fallout with ex: Was called a witch, accused of black magic Kangana Ranaut recently spoke about a difficult phase in her late 20s when she faced severe personal attacks and was falsely accused of practising black magic. She described the experience as a witch-hunt.

Sai Tamhankar: That era, when women were just ornamental, is gone Sai Tamhankar, in an exclusive interaction with India Today, reflected on the positive change in the portrayal of women in Indian cinema across genres.

Miss World? Me? Priyanka Chopra's throwback post recalling life, career goes viral Priyanka Chopra Jonas revisited her journey from childhood dreams to global stardom, reflecting on her work in films, entrepreneurship, motherhood and her move from India to Hollywood. She will be seen next in SS Rajamouli's Varanasi.

She's an electric scene partner: Laetitia Hollard on working with Katherine LaNasa Laetitia Hollard found a real-life mentor in The Pitt co-star Kaherine LaNasa. The actor, who plays nurse Emma Nolan in the Emmy-winning medical drama, opened up about the on-set bond that mirrored her character's own journey.

Actor Daisy Shah recalls being 'touched badly' by a South filmmaker Daisy Shah recalled an uncomfortable casting incident early in her career, involving inappropriate contact by a filmmaker, but chose not to name him.

Kangana Ranaut thinks Karan Johar is 'not a bad person, just superficial' Kangana Ranaut revisited her complicated equation with Karan Johar, calling him "flaky." She also looked back at an early moment of validation from Salman Khan.

Udta Teer: Ayushmann Khurrana, Sara Ali Khan's spy comedy gets a release date Ayushmann Khurrana and Sara Ali Khan are teaming up for the spy comedy Udta Teer. The film, directed by Akash A Kaushik, has locked in a release date.

Jana Nayagan leak: Freelance editor stole footage from studio, 3 arrested The Tamil Nadu Cyber Crime Wing has arrested three key suspects involved in the online leak of Jana Nayagan. This move comes amid heightened efforts to curb illegal distribution and protect the film industry from such breaches.

Exclusive: No imitating Michael, say Jaafar Jackson and director Antoine Fuqua Actor Jaafar Jackson and director Antoine Fuqua have said that the upcoming musical, Michael, does not intend to imitate Michael Jackson.

Kangana Ranaut supports 'top-most actress' Deepika Padukone's 8-hour work demand Kangana Ranaut weighed in on the debate sparked by Deepika Padukone's reported stance on an eight-hour workday, backing her right to set boundaries at this stage in her career.

Jana Nayagan leak row: Madras High Court bans illegal streaming, cable broadcast The Madras High Court has issued a temporary ban on unlawful streaming of Thalapathy Vijay's Jana Nayagan. This move aims to safeguard the big-budget project from further financial losses caused by illegal online distribution.

Yash likens Toxic to Oscar-winning film Sinners. Check out the parallel he draws Kannada star Yash awaits the release of his next film Toxic. In an interview, he called the premise unique and layered, likening it to the Oscar-winning Hollywood film Sinners.

A drop of divinity: Mahesh Bhatt says granddaughter Raha made him rediscover life Mahesh Bhatt opened up about the profound joy of being a grandfather, calling granddaughter Raha Kapoor "a drop of divinity" in his life.

Puneet Superstar to Deepak Kalal: Samay Raina's wild OTT plans that never happened Comedian Samay Raina recently revealed that he had ambitious plans to launch a creator-driven OTT platform featuring unique shows with Puneet Superstar and Deepak Kalal, but these were halted due to the India's Got Latent controversy.

Actor SJ Suryah reveals reasons for rejecting Lubber Pandhu role At the LIK success meet, actor SJ Suryah shared reasons behind declining the lead role in Lubber Pandhu, which later became a blockbuster. He spoke about trusting his instincts and praised the director's unique storytelling style.
Ek Din new trailer: Junaid Khan and Sai Pallavi find hope in magical romance The new trailer of Sai Pallavi and Junaid Khan's Ek Din promises a unique love story filled with hope and magic.

general news.headline: Trump says Israel, Lebanon agreed to 10-day truce: Solved 9 wars, this is my 10thThe US President said Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a ceasefire set to begin tonight, describing it as an initial step towards a peace process between the two sides.

Innocent, falsely implicated: TCS Nashik case accused's mother to India TodayTabassum Sheikh, mother of Danish Sheikh, co-accused in the TCS Nashik sex harassment and forced conversion case, denied all allegations against her son. "He is a very good person," she insisted.

Chanakya would've been shocked: Priyanka Gandhi to Amit Shah over women's quotaCongress's Wayanad MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra took a 'Chanakya' swipe at Union Home Minister Amit Shah during the women's quota debate. Her remark saw Union Home Minister Amit Shah smiling.

Vinod Kambli's health worsens: Tendulkar joins WhatsApp group raising supportVinod Kambli continues to fight serious health challenges, with memory issues still a concern. As support grows, Sachin Tendulkar and others have stepped in to help through a private initiative.

Truth about Pakistan, Munir revealed: Iran visit video spills beansPakistan army chief Asim Munir's Tehran visit has once again revealed who holds power in Pakistan. While Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi stood back, Munir was the first person to de-board the flight in Tehran and was greeted by Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi.

How PM Modi turned debate on women's quota into a dare for oppositionDuring the debate on the Women's Reservation Act, PM Modi adopted a classic strategy to corner the opposition. PM Modi framed the issue of women's quota as both a moral obligation and a political acid test, thereby raising the stakes for the opposition.

How Pete Hegseth brought Pulp Fiction Bible verse to PentagonAt a Pentagon prayer service, President Trump's Secretary of War, Pete Hegseth, recited what sounded like scripture, but was largely lifted from Pulp Fiction. Hegseth's use of the fictitious Bible lines, delivered in the movie by Samuel L Jackson, blurred the line between faith and fiction.

These things happen: TCS Nashik BPO HR told woman not to report harassmentExplaining the role of the two female accused, the Nashik Police Commissioner said that the operations head and the HR head of the unit had allegedly discouraged the survivor from proceeding with the complaint, saying "these things happen," while siding with the accused.

Victoria Beckham breaks silence, avoids Brooklyn's mention in cryptic commentThe Beckham family drama just won't die down. Victoria Beckham has now subtly weighed in on her fallout with son Brooklyn in a new interview without naming him.

Prabhsimran-powered Punjab stay unbeaten, hand Mumbai a reality checkPrabhsimran's 80 and a captain's knock from chase master Shreyas Iyer was enough for Punjab Kings to remain unbeaten in the IPL 2026 campaign and pile on the misery on Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium.

Massive fire ravages luxury cottages near Goa's Ajoba Temple, losses in croresA devastating fire tore through luxury cottages near Ajoba Temple in North Goa's Ashwem-Mandrem area, with flames spreading rapidly and causing massive losses while raising concerns over emergency response delays.

59 for Tamil Nadu, 30 for Kerala: BJP MP says delimitation move best deal for SouthThe key concern for the South over the Delimitation Bill is that it would cost them seats and influence. However, BJP MP Tejasvi Surya asserted that the government would increase the share of all states uniformly by 50%, which would, in fact, benefit the southern states.

Kangana Ranaut supports 'top-most actress' Deepika Padukone's 8-hour work demandKangana Ranaut weighed in on the debate sparked by Deepika Padukone's reported stance on an eight-hour workday, backing her right to set boundaries at this stage in her career.

Punjabi woman with son in US Army held for deportation; starved for 24 hoursA 53-year-old Indian-origin court interpreter in the US, who has spent over three decades helping immigrants navigate the US legal system, was detained by ICE. Despite her son serving in the US Army and having legal protection, Meenu Batra now faces deportation under a decades-old removal order.

In 37 minutes, PM turned women's quota debate into a masterclass in messagingPrime Minister Narendra Modi steered a high-stakes Lok Sabha debate on the women's quota, blending optics with sharp political messaging as the Opposition signalled resistance through notes, nudges and coordinated responses on the floor.

Meow, meow by pilots over US airport that saw a deadly mid-air crash in 2025Meows and barks crackled over an emergency ATC frequency near Virginia's Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport, baffling air traffic controllers. What might seem like harmless banter instead shows risky distractions in already strained airspace still haunted by last year's deadly mid-air collision.

MS Dhoni sparks frenzy in Hyderabad, fans throng airport for a glimpse. VideoMS Dhoni received a rousing welcome in Hyderabad as he travelled for his first away game of IPL 2026. The Chennai Super Kings star's wife shared videos of fan frenzy at the Hyderabad airport on Instagram on Thursday.

Western disturbance to hit India, no relief from heat on FridayDespite the incoming system, temperatures across north and central India are forecast to remain high. Meteorologists warn that the limited rainfall will not be sufficient to bring down the heat.

Is delimitation bill timing a BJP bid to raid Mamata's Lakshmir Bhandar?The BJP-led Centre's push to add extra seats in Parliament is pivoted on reserving 33% of the seats for women. But the timing of the special session of Parliament for introducing the bills coincides with elections in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu. Is it the BJP's bid to raid the Trinamool's Lakshmir Bhandar of women voters?

DU student alleges bias at event over sleeveless suit; not true, claim attendeesIn the clip, the Delhi University student said she had been invited to take part in a session and was also selected to felicitate the judges. However, she was barred from doing so because of her attire.

Internet chokes up over video of disabled girl smiling as her friends playA video showed a physically disabled girl smiling and laughing as she watched her friends play, a simple yet deeply moving moment that has touched hearts online.

R Ashwin backs ex-RCB pacer to be CSK's replacement for Khaleel AhmedR Ashwin has endorsed former RCB pacer Umesh Yadav as CSK's replacement for Khaleel Ahmed in the ongoing IPL 2026 season. Khaleel was injured during the KKR win and will miss the remainder of the IPL 2026 campaign.

The Rs 1 crore mistake? Why foreign degree returns aren't guaranteed anymoreMany Indian students invest lakhs in overseas education expecting quick returns. Experts say that assumption is flawed. From job uncertainty to hidden costs, the real return on investment depends on multiple factors. This story breaks down the biggest mistakes students make while calculating ROI and what they often overlook today.

No bindi, tilak, but hijab allowed? Lenskart clarifies after backlash over dress codeLenskart faced backlash over its grooming guide as people highlighted that the eyewear startup allows hijab but prohibits bindi, tilak and kalawa. Lenskart founder and CEO Peyush Bansal clarified that the company's policies had evolved, and the document didn't represent its "present guidelines".

sports.headline: Prabhsimran-powered Punjab stay unbeaten, hand Mumbai a reality check Prabhsimran's 80 and a captain's knock from chase master Shreyas Iyer was enough for Punjab Kings to remain unbeaten in the IPL 2026 campaign and pile on the misery on Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium.

Rs 1cr bargain: Quinton de Kock hits 53-ball ton in timely reminder to MI coaches IPL 2026, MI vs PBKS: Getting his first chance of the season after an injury to Rohit Sharma, Quinton de Kock proved he is not suited for warming the benches. The veteran wicketkeeper-batter smashed 112 off 60 balls, making an immediate impact for the Mumbai Indians.

Manchester City captain Bernardo Silva to leave the club at end of season Manchester City captain Bernardo Silva will depart the club at the end of the season after the expiry of his contract. Bernardo has won 19 major honours with City, including a Champions League title.

Shreyas Iyer defies gravity for relay catch in Mumbai, Rohit's reaction goes viral Shreyas Iyer stunned the Mumbai crowd on Thursday, April 16, pulling off a ridiculous save at the boundary line. Shreyas' gravity defying effort resulted in Hardik Pandya's dismissal in Mumbai Indians vs Punjab Kings.

MS Dhoni sparks frenzy in Hyderabad, fans throng airport for a glimpse. Video MS Dhoni received a rousing welcome in Hyderabad as he travelled for his first away game of IPL 2026. The Chennai Super Kings star's wife shared videos of fan frenzy at the Hyderabad airport on Instagram on Thursday.

Jay Shah named in prestigious Young Global Leaders by World Economic Forum Jay Shah has been named in the Young Global Leaders list by the World Economic Forum. The forum recognised Shah for his growing influence in global cricket and his role in expanding the sport through the International Cricket Council.

MI vs PBKS: Suryakumar fails again as India captaincy rumours continue to swirl MI vs PBKS, IPL 2026: Suryakumar Yadav was dismissed for a golden duck by Arshdeep Singh in Mumbai on Thursday. The T20 World Cup-winning captain has struggled for consistency in the new season of the IPL.

Huge blow for MI: Injured Rohit sidelined for more than one game in IPL 2026 Hardik Pandya has revealed that Rohit Sharma will be out for at least a couple of games as he failed to recover in time for the clash against PBKS.

FIFA hits back at USA for asking world body to subsidise travel costs for fans FIFA has hit back at the United States after local authorities asked the governing body to subsidise fan travel costs for the FIFA World Cup 2026. Tension has been rising over reports that matchday transport could cost close to $100.

In IPL exile, Lalit Modi places his bet on oldest sport in the world IPL founder Lalit Modi is working on his next big idea, the former cricket administrator revealed in a chat with former England captain Michael Vaughan. Modi explained his vision of creating a new sporting league, which he believed would find guaranteed success all over the world.

R Ashwin backs ex-RCB pacer to be CSK's replacement for Khaleel Ahmed R Ashwin has endorsed former RCB pacer Umesh Yadav as CSK's replacement for Khaleel Ahmed in the ongoing IPL 2026 season. Khaleel was injured during the KKR win and will miss the remainder of the IPL 2026 campaign.

MI vs PBKS Highlights: Punjab Kings thrash Mumbai Indians by 7 wicketsMumbai (MI) vs Punjab (PBKS) Highlights, IPL 2026: PBKS (198/3 in 16.3 ovs). Shreyas Iyer and Prabhsimran Singh have guided Punjab Kings to a terrific victory over Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium. With the win, Punjab remain unbeaten in the tournament.

After Cummins return news, SRH dealt new injury blow as pacer ruled of IPL 2026Sunrisers Hyderabad have been dealt a new injury blow as overseas pacer David Payne has been ruled out of the remainder of the IPL 2026 season. Hyderabad are set to welcome back Pat Cummins into the mix after his back issue was resolved.

Lalit Modi explains why IND vs PAK bilaterals may never happenLalit Modi says political tensions and a "politicised" PCB are key reasons behind the absence of India vs Pakistan bilaterals, adding he does not see a revival happening anytime soon.

MS Dhoni back? CSK great sets off to Hyderabad, raises comeback hopesIPL 2026: After opting not to travel to Guwahati and Bengaluru for their previous away games, MS Dhoni joined the Chennai Super KIngs squad which left for Hyderabad on Thursday. CSK will play SunRisers Hyderabad on Saturday, April 18.

RCB's golden era? Bangar predicts title spree for Royal Challengers BengaluruSanjay Bangar sees RCB entering a golden era after their maiden IPL title, backing the in-form side to build a winning culture and challenge for multiple championships in the coming years.

Messi in legal trouble over missed Argentina friendlyLionel Messi is facing legal trouble over a missed Argentina friendly in the US. A Miami-based promoter claims his absence led to major financial losses under a multi-million dollar deal.

Vinod Kambli's health worsens: Tendulkar joins WhatsApp group raising supportVinod Kambli continues to fight serious health challenges, with memory issues still a concern. As support grows, Sachin Tendulkar and others have stepped in to help through a private initiative.

IPL: MI rope in Punjab all-rounder Krish Bhagat to replace Atharva AnkolekarMumbai Indians sign Krish Bhagat as replacement for injured Atharva Ankolekar in IPL 2026, with the Punjab all-rounder joining at base price as MI look to revive a slow start.

Vaishali's rise to Candidates champion earns Judith Polgar's praiseR Vaishali's stunning run to Women's Candidates title draws praise from Judit and Susan Polgar on X, as the lowest-rated player seals a World Championship spot with a final-round win.
CSK's Khaleel Ahmed ruled out of IPL 2026 due to injuryIPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings have been dealt a big blow for their ongoing season with their pacer, Khaleel Ahmed being ruled out due to injury.

Rasikh always had it: RCB teammate credits pacer's experience for LSG heroicsIPL 2026: Rasikh Salam's four-wicket haul against LSG drew praise, but not surprise, from Jitesh Sharma. The RCB keeper insisted the pacer always had the skillset, with experience and guidance now helping him deliver consistently.

Rohit Sharma fitness doubt adds to MI's worries against rampant PBKSIPL 2026, MI vs PBKS: Mumbai Indians have struggled so far, losing three matches on the trot, and face a tough challenge against the unbeaten Punjab Kings on Thursday at the Wankhede Stadium. Rohit Sharma's injury concern and Jasprit Bumrah's indifferent form have only added to their worries.

CSK writes to BCCI over 'Dosa Idly' song jibe at Chinnaswamy during RCB clashCSK write to BCCI over 'Dosa Idly' song and DJ remarks at Chinnaswamy during IPL 2026 clash vs RCB, raising concerns over match-day conduct and rivalry tensions.

Vaishali's Candidates triumph: A win vs odds that redefines the underdog termLowest-rated in the field, counted out before it began, Vaishali still found a way. This wasn't just a win, it was a grind through chaos, pressure and moments that could have ended it all.

Pat Cummins cleared for IPL 2026 return: Will he take part in SRH vs CSK?IPL 2026: Pat Cummins has been cleared to return after recovering from his back injury. However, he is unlikely to feature in SRH's clash against CSK as he targets a later comeback.

Frustrated Real Madrid slam referee over soft Camavinga red vs BayernReal Madrid's UCL exit was overshadowed by controversy over Camavinga's red card. The decision proved decisive as Bayern capitalised late to seal progression.

IPL Play Of The Day: Rasikh Salam beats Mukul Choudhary in duel, earns RCB trustIPL 2026: Rasikh Salam came into his own for RCB and defeated the big-hitting Mukul Choudhary in a last-over Mexican standoff. With RCB finally finding their third seamer, Rasikh could be the difference in their run towards a second consecutive title.

R Vaishali's Checkmate: From 'Pragg's sister' to World Championship challengerLong the other Rameshbabu in a house of genius, the elder sibling has finally pulled rank. By conquering the Candidates in Cyprus, R Vaishali secured a World Championship date with China's Ju Wenjun, and a name of her own.

KKR should open with Green: Aaron Finch on all-rounder's struggles in IPL 2026IPL 2026: Cameron Green has struggled since returning from injury and being used out of position, with poor performances in both batting and bowling adding to KKR's concerns.

Not as versatile as Bumrah, but as effective: Behind Josh Hazlewood's IPL successRoyal Challengers Bengaluru fast bowler Josh Hazlewood admitted he does not possess the same range of variations as Jasprit Bumrah, but said his success in the Indian Premier League has come from sticking to accuracy and a simple method.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
